title: Identify unit fractions
description: Identify unit fractions when given a visual or a context.
topic_path: Module 5: Fractions as numbers on the number line
Topic A: Partitioning a whole into equal parts

assessment_items.question_content:
The sun hits $1$ of the $4$ equal-sized sections of an ice skating rink more than the others.  This section is extra slippery because the sun has melted it some.

[[☃ image 1]]

 
**What fraction of the ice skating rink is extra slippery because of the sun?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $4$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $4$.
When we have $4$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the total area.
Since the sun hits $1$ section more than the others, we say that  $\dfrac{1}{4}$ , or one-fourth, of the ice skating rink is extra slippery.

options.alt: A circle divided into four equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scarlett cuts the grass in her grandparents' yard.  Her grandparents' yard is made up of $2$ equal-sized sections.  So far, Scarlett has cut the grass in $1$ section.     

[[☃ image 1]]

 
**What fraction of the grass has Scarlett cut?**   

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $2$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $2$.
When we have $2$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the total area.
Since Scarlett has only cut $1$ section, we say that she has cut $\dfrac{1}{2}$ , or one-half, of the grass.

options.alt: A square divided into two equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Denis baked a peach pie and cut it into $3$ equal-sized pieces.  Denis's dad eats $1$ section of the pie.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction of the pie did Denis's dad eat?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $3$ pieces, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $3$.
When we have $3$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the total area.
Since Denis's dad only ate $1$ piece, we say that  Denis's dad ate $\dfrac{1}{3}$ , or one-third, of the peach pie.

options.alt: A circle divided into three equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vera's dinner plate is divided into $3$ equal-sized sections.  Vera puts all of her broccoli in $1$ section.   

[[☃ image 1]]

 
**What fraction of the plate has broccoli?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $3$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $3$.
When we have $3$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the total area.
Since the broccoli is only in $1$ section, we say that  $\dfrac{1}{3}$ , or one-third, of the plate has broccoli.

options.alt: A circle divided into three equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Min-Jun is painting $3$ equal-sized sections of his canvas.  So far, he has painted $1$ section.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction of the canvas has Min-Jun painted?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $3$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $3$.
When we have $3$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the total area.
Since Min-Jun has only painted $1$ section, we say that he has painted $\dfrac{1}{3}$ , or one-third, of the canvas.

options.alt: A rectangle divided into three equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mrs. Patterson uses chalk to split the blacktop into $3$ equal-sized sections.  She says students playing soccer can only play in $1$ section. 

[[☃ image 1]]

 
 
**What fraction of the blacktop is open to students playing soccer?**   

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $3$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $3$.
When we have $3$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the total area.
Since only $1$ section allows soccer, we say that $\dfrac{1}{3}$ , or one-third, of the blacktop is available to soccer players.

options.alt: A rectangle divided into three equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
A farmer noticed that out of his $4$ equal-sized fields, only $1$ has sprouted.  

[[☃ image 1]]

 
**What fraction of the fields have sprouted?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $4$ fields, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $4$.
When we have $4$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the total area.
Since only $1$ field has sprouted, we say that $\dfrac{1}{4}$ , or one-fourth, of the fields has sprouted.

options.alt: A rectangle divided into four equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chloe cuts the lasagna into $4$ equal-sized pieces.  Oh no!  Her dog eats $1$ section. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction of the lasagna did her dog eat?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $4$ pieces, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $4$.
When we have $4$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the total area.
Since her dog only eats $1$ piece, we say that her dog ate $\dfrac{1}{4}$ , or one-fourth, of the lasagna.

options.alt: A square divided into four equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
For the birthday party, Rafaela is splitting the table into $2$ equal-sized sections.  All of the desserts will go on $1$ section of the table.  

[[☃ image 1]]

 
**What fraction of the table will hold desserts?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $2$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $2$.
When we have $2$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the total area.
Since desserts will only go on $1$ section, we say that  $\dfrac{1}{2}$ , or one-half, of the table will hold desserts.

options.alt: A rectangle divided into two equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vitor tries to throw a frisbee to Kaylee but instead it lands in the mud.  When Kaylee picks up the frisbee she sees that the muddy part and non-muddy part of the frisbee are the same size.  

The mud covers $1$ of $2$ equal-sized sections of the frisbee.

[[☃ image 1]]

 
**What fraction of the frisbee is covered in mud?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: There are $2$ sections, and they are all the same size. The total number of equal parts is $2$.
When we have $2$ equal parts, then the area of each part is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the total area.
Since the mud only covers $1$ section, we say that  $\dfrac{1}{2}$ , or one-half, of the frisbee is covered in mud.

options.alt: A circle divided into two equal parts with one part shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction of the area of the rectangle is shaded?**    

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: The rectangle is split into $2$ equal parts. $1$ of the parts of the rectangle is shaded.
The area of each part of the rectangle is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the area of the whole.
The shaded part is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the area.

options.alt: A rectangle divided into two equal parts with one part shaded.

The area of each part of the circle is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the area of the whole.
The area of the shaded part is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ .

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction of the cookie is $1$ piece?**  

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The cookie is divided into $\greenD2$ equal-sized parts.



[[☃ image 1]]
When we divide a whole into $\greenD2$ equal-sized parts, each part is $\dfrac{1}{\greenD2}$ of the whole.
Each piece is $\dfrac{1}{\greenD2}$ of the whole cookie.

options.alt: A cookie divided into two equal-sized pieces.

choices.content: $\dfrac11$
$\dfrac22$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction of the cake is $1$ piece?**  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The cake is divided into $\greenD2$ equal-sized parts, or $\greenD{\text{halves}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
When we divide a whole into $\greenD2$ equal-sized parts, each part is $1\greenD{\text{ half}}$ of the whole.
Each piece is $1\greenD{\text{ half}}$ of the whole cake.

options.alt: A cake divided into two equal pieces.

choices.content: $1$ half
$1$ one
$2$ halves

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the whole.

This figure is divided into $3$ equal-sized pieces, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of the whole.

This figure is divided into $2$ equal-sized pieces, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of the whole.

This figure has $\dfrac12$ of its area shaded:

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which figure has $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of its area shaded?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This figure is divided into $4$ equal-sized pieces, with $1$  piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the whole.

This figure is divided into $6$ equal-sized pieces, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $\dfrac{1}{6}$ of the whole.

This figure is divided into $8$ equal-sized pieces, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of the whole.

This figure has $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of its area shaded:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A rectangle divided into four equal parts with one part shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/88d41c121244ea697184f00cdb207a828b41d97d)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which figure has $1\text{ eighth}$ of its area shaded?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This figure is divided into $3$ equal-sized pieces, or $\text{thirds}$, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $1\text{ third}$ of the whole.

This figure is divided into $4$ equal-sized pieces, or $\text{fourths}$, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $1\text{ fourth}$ of the whole.

This figure is divided into $8$ equal-sized pieces, or $\text{eighths}$, with $1$ piece shaded.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The shaded area is $1\text{ eighth}$ of the whole.

This figure has $1\text{ eighth}$ of its area shaded:

[[☃ image 1]]
